tldr: Donald Trump announced that Iran has agreed to peace talks in Doha following recent hostilities with the US.
The European Union and China will hold three months of talks to address their significant trade imbalance, aiming to prevent a trade war.
The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both closed higher, fueled by a rebound in chip stocks and positive M&A sentiment.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed higher, primarily driven by a rebound in chip stocks and positive sentiment from M&A activities, including Comcast's NBCUniversal spinoff, as well as Alphabet's rise on its Dow debut.

yield_10y.why: The 10-year Treasury yield remained unchanged, with the Supreme Court's decision allowing Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook to stay in her post, reinforcing perceptions of central bank independence.

vix.why: The VIX, a measure of market volatility, decreased, likely reflecting the positive market close driven by tech and chip stock rebounds, and increased geopolitical stability following reports of a deal between the US and Iran to halt attacks.

tech.summary: Google has warned that the European Union's plans to weaken its market monopoly, which include requiring data sharing with competitors and opening up AI on Android, could expose user data and pose major privacy risks.
A quantum computing startup has announced ambitious plans to achieve thousands of error-corrected qubits by 2029, a development that would represent a significant leap forward for the industry.

world.summary: Donald Trump claimed that Iran has agreed to hold peace talks in Doha next week, following recent exchanges of fire between the US and Tehran in the Strait of Hormuz.
The European Union and China have agreed to a three-month period of talks to address the EU's €360bn trade deficit with China, in an effort to avoid a potential trade war.
